outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Iran and U.S. are close to an understanding aimed at ending the war, officials say
outlets.summary: Washington Post reports US and Iran are close to a framework agreement, with Qatari team in Tehran and Pakistan's Munir completing marathon mediation. Hormuz reopening and sanctions relief are central to the emerging deal.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump meeting with Gulf leaders as he weighs latest Iran proposal to end the war
outlets.summary: CNN reports Trump held a conference call with Gulf leaders who urged him to hold off on resuming strikes, as the White House weighs Iran's latest peace proposal ahead of Trump's self-imposed Sunday deadline.

outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.headline: Trump says US is "getting a lot closer" to agreement with Iran
outlets.summary: CBS News covers Trump's remarks that talks are progressing while he insists on US demands — toll-free Hormuz, no nuclear weapon, enriched uranium transfer. Trump: "I will only sign a deal where we get everything we want."

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump delays Iran attack as diplomatic push intensifies: Military ready at 'moment's notice'
outlets.summary: Fox News live updates track Trump's '50/50' ultimatum — deal or resumed strikes — as White House Deputy Chief Dan Scavino posts a B-2 stealth bomber video and US military remains at high readiness. Gulf leaders lobbied Trump to hold off.

outlets.name: Washington Times
outlets.headline: EXCLUSIVE: U.S., Iran to announce peace deal within 24 hours
outlets.summary: Washington Times reports a peace deal draft has been approved by Vance, Witkoff, Kushner, and Iran's Ghalibaf, and has been sent to Trump and Iranian leadership for final approval, with an announcement expected by Sunday.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Rubio says there's a 'chance' Iran accepts a deal as soon as Saturday
outlets.summary: The Hill reports Secretary of State Rubio speaking from New Delhi said there is "a chance" of a deal "as soon as Saturday," while reiterating US red lines: toll-free Hormuz, no nuclear weapon, HEU transfer out of Iran.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war live: President Trump says peace talks 'getting a lot closer'
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera Day 85 liveblog covers Trump's '50/50' ultimatum, Iran's 'deep and significant differences' statement, Pakistan's Munir concluding Tehran mediation, and the Washington Times peace deal exclusive — alongside Hezbollah killing IDF soldier Hamburger in northern Israel.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: US and Iran 'getting a lot closer' to an agreement to end war, Trump says
outlets.summary: Euronews reports Trump's optimistic framing of US-Iran diplomacy amid competing signals: Washington Times reporting an imminent deal, Iran FM saying differences remain 'deep,' and France drafting a competing UN resolution on Hormuz.

outlets.name: CNBC
outlets.headline: U.S. and Iran closing in on 60-day ceasefire extension with nuclear framework, FT reports
outlets.summary: CNBC, citing the Financial Times, reports mediators believe a deal on a 60-day ceasefire extension and nuclear talks framework is near. The framework would include gradual Hormuz reopening, sanctions easing, and frozen assets phased release — nuclear dismantlement deferred.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump says '50/50' Iran deal reached or US launches strikes, will likely decide by tomorrow
outlets.summary: Times of Israel Day 85 liveblog covers Trump's ultimatum, Netanyahu's emergency coalition meeting over the 'very bad' emerging deal, and Hezbollah killing IDF Staff Sgt. Noam Hamburger by drone in northern Israel — 9th soldier killed since Lebanon ceasefire.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Trump says there's a 50/50 chance of signing deal as US, Iran reportedly near ceasefire extension
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post covers the critical Day 85 decision window, reporting on the FT's 60-day ceasefire extension story and Netanyahu's displeasure that Israel was excluded from negotiations and is being pressured not to strike independently.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Report: U.S.-Iran Cease-fire May Be Extended by 60 Days, According to Mediators
outlets.summary: Haaretz reports on the dual realities of Day 85: US military staff canceling weekend plans and a 60-day ceasefire extension potentially being finalized simultaneously, reflecting the extreme uncertainty at the negotiating endgame.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: LIVE — Trump puts odds of Iran deal at '50/50' as Pakistan cites progress in talks
outlets.summary: Iran International's Day 85 liveblog tracks the competing signals: Trump's '50/50' threat, Pakistan ISPR's 'encouraging progress' statement, Iran's insistence on nuclear non-negotiability, and the Hormuz deal framework under active discussion.

outlets.name: Fortune / CGTN
outlets.headline: Iran, US working to finalize memorandum of understanding — Iranian FM spokesman
outlets.summary: Iran's FM spokesman Baghaei confirmed the two sides are working toward a 14-clause MOU. Nuclear issues would not be part of the initial agreement — those are deferred to a subsequent 30-60 day negotiation period. He cautioned: "We cannot necessarily say we have reached a point where a deal is near."

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Iran remains steadfast on nuclear rights as US-Iran MOU takes shape
outlets.summary: Press TV emphasizes Iran's non-negotiable nuclear enrichment rights as the central obstacle to any comprehensive deal. Iran FM Araghchi called on Trump to demonstrate good faith by releasing frozen Iranian assets before expecting concessions on Hormuz.
